SELECT value, sqrt(value) AS square_root FROM numbers WHERE value >= 0;
CREATE TABLE results AS SELECT value, sqrt(value) AS square_root FROM numbers WHERE value >= 0;
SELECT * FROM results WHERE square_root IS NOT NULL;
SELECT value, CASE WHEN value >= 0 THEN sqrt(value) ELSE NULL END AS square_root FROM numbers;
SELECT avg(sqrt(value)) AS average_sqrt FROM numbers WHERE value >= 0;
SELECT count(*) AS count_valid_sqrt FROM numbers WHERE value >= 0;
SELECT SUM(sqrt(value)) AS total_sqrt FROM (SELECT value FROM numbers WHERE value >= 0) as valid_numbers;
SELECT A.value, B.square_root FROM numbers AS A JOIN (SELECT value, sqrt(value) AS square_root FROM numbers WHERE value >= 0) AS B ON A.value = B.value;
